title: Hook Registry
description: JSON Schema for manifests/hook-registry.json. Classifies every hook file on disk as one of: globally-live (registered in ~/.claude/settings.json), project-installed (shipped via templates/<subsystem>/settings*.snippet*), library_modules (shared helpers like _appsec-common.js that are not directly registered), dormant_opt_in (file exists but intentionally not wired), or deprecated. The tools/hooks/lint.js script asserts: (a) every hook in `global_live` is referenced by command in settings.json, (b) every hook in `project_installed.<subsystem>.hooks` is referenced in the corresponding subsystem snippet, (c) every hook file on disk is classified somewhere (no orphans). Authoritative reference: docs/CANONICALS.md §1 D3.

global_live.description: Hooks registered in ~/.claude/settings.json (fire on every session globally)

owner_subsystem.description: Subsystem that owns the globally-live hook set (typically 'gsd' for the GSD pipeline)

settings_path.description: Path to the settings.json file these hooks are registered in (default: ~/.claude/settings.json)

project_installed.description: Per-subsystem hooks that are ONLY active when the subsystem template is installed into a project (gated by config presence). Per CANONICALS §1 D3: fresh projects without the subsystem config have NO subsystem-hook enforcement.

library_modules.description: Shared helper modules required by hooks but not registered themselves (e.g. _appsec-common.js, _qa-common.js, _uiux-common.js, _disc-common.js)

dormant_opt_in.description: Hook files that exist on disk but are intentionally not wired (kept for opt-in or future activation)

opt_in_via.description: How to enable this hook if desired

deprecated.description: Hook names that are deprecated; tests should fail if they are referenced in any settings or snippet

name.description: Hook identifier (typically the filename minus extension, e.g. gsd-prompt-guard)

path.description: Relative path under ~/.claude/ to the hook file

matcher.description: Tool matcher pattern from settings.json (Bash | Edit|Write | * etc.)

command_pattern.description: Substring or regex fragment that must appear in the settings.json command for this hook to be considered registered. Used by tools/hooks/lint.js for reference verification.

blocking.description: Whether this hook can exit non-zero to block the action

snippet_path.description: Relative path to the snippet that ships with this subsystem (e.g. templates/discoverability/settings-snippet.json)

install_location_hint.description: Where project authors merge the snippet (typically <project>/.claude/settings.json)

config_gate.description: Config file whose presence enables the hooks (e.g. discoverability.config.yaml). Without it, hooks silent-exit.

source_dir.description: Where the hook source files live (typically templates/<subsystem>/hooks/ or ~/.claude/hooks/)

path.description: Relative path to the hook file. Tests check this file actually exists.

command_pattern.description: Substring that must appear in the corresponding snippet's command field. Used by tools/hooks/lint.js.

disable_via_config.description: Config key to disable just this hook (e.g. harness.hook_modes.deploy_gate = off)
